title: Arrow Functions 🏹
description: Write faster, modern, one-line functions like a pro.

slides.title: The Modern Shorthand 🏎️
The Arrow Syntax 🏹
slides.content:
## Writing Your First Arrow

Here is how you write the same function in the modern arrow syntax:

```javascript
const addNumber = (a, b) => {
  return a + b;
}
```

1. Use `const` and the function name.
2. Put the parameters in parentheses `()`.
3. Add the arrow `=>`.
4. Write the block `{}`.

It still acts exactly the same: `addNumber(2, 2)` outputs `4`.

slides.title: Implicit Return Magic! ✨
slides.content:
## Getting Even Shorter

The real power of arrow functions happens when your function is only **one line of code long**.

If it's just one line, you can completely remove the curly braces `{}` AND you can completely remove the `return` keyword! 

JavaScript will automatically (implicitly) return whatever is on that line.

```javascript
// Tiny, one-line perfection!
const addNumber = (a, b) => a + b;
```

Wow! That's clean! 🧼

slides.title: Try It Out 💻
slides.content: See how much cleaner logic looks with arrow functions and implicit returns! Very easy to read.

quizData.question: What happens if you use curly braces {} with an arrow function, but forget to write the 'return' keyword inside them?

options.text: It implicitly returns the last line automatically
It acts like a normal variable

quizData.explanation: Implicit return ONLY works if you omit the curly braces. If you type the curly braces, you MUST type the word 'return', just like a traditional function!

slides.title: Chapter Summary! 🎉
slides.content:
## You Finished Chapter 6!

You're writing real, modular code now!

You've learned:

* How to define and call reusable `function` blocks.
* How to pass data IN using Parameters and Arguments.
* How to pass data OUT using `return`.
* How Local Scope prevents variable collision out in the Global world.
* How to write beautiful ES6 Arrow Functions!

Up next: Chapter 7, where we finally learn how to store HUGE lists of data using Arrays and Objects! 🗃️
